title: Gradient descent from scratch
context: Built a from-scratch gradient descent solver for a 50-dimensional engineering cost function as part of the Optimization Methods course.
details: Implemented vanilla, momentum, and Adam variants in NumPy without relying on SciPy's optimizers. Used analytical gradients derived symbolically and verified numerically against a finite-difference check.
outcome: Converged to within 1e-4 of the reference optimum in under 2000 iterations on the cost function.

title: Genetic algorithm from scratch
context: Built a genetic algorithm solver to compare against the gradient descent baseline on the same cost function.
details: Implemented tournament selection, blend crossover, and Gaussian mutation. Tuned population size, mutation rate, and crossover probability against the same 50-dimensional problem.
outcome: Reached within 1% of the gradient descent optimum after 500 generations.

title: CPU vs GPU benchmark on matrix operations
context: Compared NumPy CPU and a hand-written CUDA kernel on the same matrix-multiplication workload.
details: Wrote a CUDA kernel via CuPy for matrix multiplication. Measured wall-clock time for matrix sizes from 256x256 to 4096x4096. Documented the breakeven matrix size where GPU begins to win.
outcome: Recorded an 8x speedup on large matrices above 2048x2048 on the workstation GPU.

hpc.emphasis: Benchmarked a hand-written CuPy/CUDA kernel against NumPy on matrix multiplication, documenting an 8x speedup on matrices larger than 2048x2048 on the workstation GPU.

title: Profiled memory bottlenecks with cProfile
context: Ran cProfile and tracemalloc on the optimization pipeline to surface memory hot spots.
details: Found that the per-iteration cost-function evaluation allocated a fresh temporary array on every step. Replaced it with a pre-allocated buffer reused across iterations.
outcome: Reduced peak RAM usage by 35% via generator-based streaming of intermediate results.

title: Generator-based streaming of intermediates
context: Replaced eager intermediate-result lists with Python generators to reduce peak memory during optimization runs.
details: The original pipeline materialized every iteration's intermediate arrays in memory. The generator-based version yields one iteration's result at a time and lets the consumer drop it after use.
outcome: 35% reduction in peak RAM as measured by tracemalloc.

title: Presented findings to 25-person seminar
context: Delivered a 20-minute talk covering the two optimizers, the GPU benchmark, and the memory profiling results.
details: Used Jupyter slides with embedded live benchmarks. Took questions on the GPU breakeven analysis and on whether the genetic algorithm would benefit from a different crossover operator.
outcome: Top marks on both the technical report and the seminar presentation.

title: Authored technical report with benchmarking section
context: Wrote up the methodology, the CPU-vs-GPU benchmark, and the memory profiling findings in a seminar report.
details: Structured the report to mirror the structure of the seminar talk. Embedded the benchmark plots directly from the Jupyter notebook so they remained reproducible.
outcome: Top marks on the technical report.

title: Convergence analysis of the two optimizers
context: Plotted cost vs iteration for gradient descent and the genetic algorithm on the same cost function.
details: Recorded the cost at every iteration for both optimizers. Gradient descent plateaued by iteration 800; the genetic algorithm required the full 500 generations to reach 1% of the GD optimum.
outcome: Quantified the convergence trade-off between local and global search methods on the chosen cost function.

title: Wrote a CUDA kernel via CuPy RawKernel
context: Used CuPy's RawKernel to write a hand-tuned CUDA kernel for matrix multiplication.
details: Implemented a tiled matrix-multiplication kernel with shared-memory blocking. Compiled and benchmarked against the stock cuBLAS-backed matmul.
outcome: Reached 70% of cuBLAS performance on 2048x2048 matrices.

title: Summarized memory optimization techniques
context: Wrote a 'lessons learned' section in the report covering the memory optimization techniques that worked.
details: Compared pre-allocation, generator streaming, and in-place NumPy operations. Documented the trade-off between code clarity and memory savings for each.
outcome: Provided reusable guidance for the next optimization assignment.

title: Hyperparameter sweep for the genetic algorithm
context: Ran a small grid sweep over population size, mutation rate, and crossover probability.
details: Used a 5x3x3 grid. Recorded best final cost for each combination. Plotted a heat map of the best configurations.
outcome: Identified a configuration that converged 30% faster than the default.

title: Literature review on gradient methods
context: Read four chapters and two papers on first-order optimization methods to position the implementation against the state of the art.
details: Compared the implemented Adam variant to the published baseline. Noted the small but consistent gap on the chosen cost function.
outcome: Cited prior work in the report's related-work section.

title: Met weekly with the course supervisor
context: Held weekly check-ins with the Optimization Methods supervisor.
details: Brought benchmark plots and draft sections to each meeting. Used feedback to prioritize the CUDA work over a neural-network-based alternative.
outcome: Scoped the project to fit the seminar timeline.

title: Maintained project in Git
context: Hosted the project on GitHub with topic branches separating optimizers, benchmarks, and report-writing.
details: Tagged the report submission as v1.0 so the supervisor could fetch a reproducible snapshot.
outcome: Provided a reproducible snapshot for the seminar submission.
